events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess confined space permit. Scenario: oxygen 20.9% logged 30 min ago. LEL, H2S, CO fields blank. Meter bump-test record for today not attached. Attendant and rescue plan complete. Stage planned. Determine whether entry can be authorized as documented. Identify missing. Under OSHA 1910.146, permit must specify acceptable entry conditions, including results of initial atmospheric testing (oxygen, flammable gases/vapors, toxic). Also requires calibration of direct-reading portable gas monitor; bump test? OSHA requires calibration per manufacturer; many standards require bump test before each day's use. Permit incomplete missing LEL, H2S, CO readings and bump test record. Oxygen reading 30 min old may be stale; need current pre-entry testing. Decision fail. Risk high/critical? Missing atmospheric hazards and gas monitor verification is critical. Let's set critical. Work_stage planned. percent_complete 0. value maybe oxygen 20.9. component_conditions empty. findings list. actions. references. Need valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

    "Permit is missing required atmospheric test results for LEL, H2S, and CO.",
    "Only oxygen is recorded and the reading is 30 minutes old; pre-entry atmospheric tests must be current.",
    "Today's bump-test/calibration record for the gas monitor is not attached; monitor suitability is unverified.",
    "Incomplete atmospheric data means acceptable entry conditions cannot be confirmed."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Complete atmospheric testing for oxygen, LEL, H2S, and CO immediately before entry and record all results on the permit.",
    "Attach the gas monitor's current bump-test/calibration record to the permit.",
    "Verify the monitor is functioning and calibrated per manufacturer requirements.",
    "Reauthorize the permit only after all required fields and test records are complete and within acceptable limits."
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1910.146 requires the permit to document acceptable entry conditions, including current atmospheric test results for oxygen, flammables, and toxics, and requires a properly calibrated direct-reading instrument. The permit as written lacks LEL, H2S, and CO readings, relies on a 30-minute-old oxygen reading, and has no bump-test record, so entry cannot be authorized.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(C) - permit specifies acceptable entry conditions",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(d)(5) - testing and monitoring the internal atmosphere",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(F) - equipment required for safe entry",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(H) - rescue and emergency services"
  ]
